rulings.Case Title: SUCCESSOR AGENCY TO THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY OF TH ET AL VS. ALL PERSONS CLAIMING ANY INTEREST IN OR LIEN UPON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 19, 2015 line 4. PLAINTIFF SUCCESSOR AGENCY TO THE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED. Moving papers establish that the Lebolt Defendants' predecessor in interest could not possibly have gained title to the property in question at the 1914 tax sale, because the land was in fact owned by the State of California, subject only to a right-of-way easement deeded to the railroad companies in 1868. The fact that the railways accepted this deed is conclusively established by Southern Pacific Co. v. City & County of San Francisco (1964) 62 Cal.2d 50, 52.) Because the State owned the land in question at the time of the 1914 tax sale, as a matter of law neither the tax sale nor the subsequent 1917 quiet title action could have divested the State of ownership. As such, the Lebolt Defendants have no possible valid claim to the property. The City is not equitably estopped from asserting this interest by the 1914 tax sale - the City's interest is derived from the State's sovereign claim to the lands. (See Big Valley Band of Pomo Indians v. Superior Court (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1185, 1196-96 (sovereign immunity cannot be overridden by equitable principles).) The prevailing parties shall prepare an order and judgment approved as to form. = (501/REQ)
